phase1.explanation:
Generics provide type safety and code reuse:

Generic Classes:
• class Box<T> — T is a type parameter
• Can be used with any type: Box<String>, Box<Integer>
• Type checking at compile time

Multiple Type Parameters:
• class Pair<K, V> — two type parameters
• K for key, V for value

Generic Methods:
• <T> void printArray(T[] array) — type parameter before return
• Type inferred from arguments

Wildcards (?):
• <?> — unknown type (any type)
• <? extends Number> — any subclass of Number (upper bounded)
• <? super Integer> — any superclass of Integer (lower bounded)

Bounded Type Parameters:
• <T extends Number> — T must be Number or subclass
• <T extends Comparable<T>> — T must be comparable

Benefits of Generics:
• Type safety — compiler catches type errors
• No casting needed — cleaner code
• Code reuse — one class works with multiple types

⚠️ Type Erasure:
• Generic types are removed at runtime
• Cannot use primitive types (use wrappers)

⚠️ Limitations:
• Cannot create generic arrays
• Cannot instantiate generic types
• Cannot use instanceof with generic types

phase2.errorMessage:
Test.java:5: error: unexpected type
        ArrayList<int> list = new ArrayList<>();  // ERROR!
                   ^
  required: reference type
  found:    int
1 error
phase2.explanation:
Generics only work with reference types, not primitives!

You can't use ArrayList<int>, ArrayList<double>, etc.

FIX: Use wrapper classes:
ArrayList<Integer> list = new ArrayList<>();  // ✓
ArrayList<Double> list2 = new ArrayList<>();  // ✓

Java automatically handles autoboxing/unboxing:
list.add(42);   // int → Integer (autoboxing)
int value = list.get(0);  // Integer → int (unboxing)

Remember: Generics = reference types only!

phase3.prompt:
Create a generic class Container<T> with:
• Private field: item (T type)
• Constructor that takes T
• Getter: get()
• Setter: set(T item)
• Method: isSame(Container<T> other) — returns true if items are equal

In main:
1. Create Container<String> with "Hello"
2. Create Container<Integer> with 42
3. Create Container<String> with "Hello"
4. Check if the two String containers are the same using isSame()
5. Print all items
